title: rubus silvaticus var. microphyllus
intro: Rubus silvaticus var. microphyllus, also known as dwarf thimbleberry or small-leaved thimbleberry, is a variety of the thimbleberry plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. The plant is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus silvaticus var. microphyllus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
